title: Compaction threshold_tokens_for uses lossy integer math and compact() cannot rollback on verify-failure
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Functional Correctness (1)

Location: src/compaction.rs:592-597 threshold_tokens_for; 423-438 compact_with_hint.

Violation 1 — lossy threshold math: (max_context_tokens / 1000 * ratio_millths) loses precision. For 16385 (GPT-3.5) * 0.85 true = 13927 but (16385/1000)*850 = 13600. Under-computes threshold by 327 tokens, triggering compaction earlier than configured. The comment blaming usize-as-f32 precision loss is wrong — f64 has 53-bit mantissa, plenty for any realistic context size.

Violation 2 — no rollback on post-compact verify failure: at line 424 request.messages is OVERWRITTEN. If line 429 sees new_tokens >= original_tokens, function returns Err but the request has ALREADY been mutated. Caller sees both a corrupted request AND an error — worst of both worlds.

Violation 3 — target_tokens = threshold_tokens/2 is an arbitrary heuristic unrelated to how much actually needs freeing.

Mandated Refactor:
1. Use f64 arithmetic: (max_context_tokens as f64 * threshold as f64).round() as usize.
2. Snapshot request.messages BEFORE line 424; restore on post-check failure.
3. target_tokens = current_tokens.saturating_sub(tokens_to_free); if insufficient, iterate up to 3 passes.
4. Tests: context 16385 threshold 0.85 == 13927 (not 13600); mutated-request-with-error case asserts request restored to original.
